lesson: In binary search use lo,hi=0,len(a)-1 and mid=(lo+hi)//2 with lo<=hi to avoid infinite loops.
Track first/second max in one pass with two variables instead of sorting the whole list.
Merge two sorted lists with a two-pointer walk, appending the smaller head each step, then the leftover tail.
Use heapq.nsmallest(k, data)[-1] to get the kth smallest without a full O(n log n) sort.
Use heapq.nlargest(n, data, key=...) to get top-n by a key in O(n log k) instead of sorting everything.
Use bisect.bisect_left(sorted_list, x) to find the sorted insertion index in O(log n), avoiding manual loops.
Sort intervals by start, then merge by comparing each next start to the running merged interval's end.
Quickselect: partition around a random pivot and recurse only into the side containing the kth index for average O(n).
In a rotated sorted array, binary search by checking which half (lo..mid or mid..hi) is properly sorted before recursing.
